<path>/Users/bill/code/go/src/github.com/ardanlabs/kronk/examples/talks/tictactoe/main.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package main
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"bufio"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"strconv"
8: 	"strings"
9: 	"time"
10: )
11: 
12: type scores struct {
13: 	X     int
14: 	O     int
15: 	Draws int
16: }
17: 
18: func clearScreen() {
19: 	fmt.Print("\033[2J\033[H")
20: }
21: 
22: func renderBoard(b [9]string, sc scores, highlight []int, blink bool) {
23: 	var sb strings.Builder
24: 
25: 	sb.WriteString("\n")
26: 	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("\033[37mScore: X: %d | O: %d | Draws: %d\033[0m\n\n", sc.X, sc.O, sc.Draws))
27: 
28: 	for i := 0; i < 9; i++ {
29: 		if i == 3 || i == 6 {
30: 			sb.WriteString("----------\n")
31: 		}
32: 		if i%3 == 0 && i > 0 {
33: 			sb.WriteString("\n")
34: 		}
35: 
36: 		val := b[i]
37: 		isHighlight := false
38: 		for _, h := range highlight {
39: 			if h == i {
40: 				isHighlight = true
41: 				break
42: 			}
43: 		}
44: 
45: 		switch val {
46: 		case "X":
47: 			if isHighlight && blink {
48: 				sb.WriteString("\033[5m\033[31;1mX\033[0m")
49: 			} else {
50: 				sb.WriteString("\033[31;1mX\033[0m")
51: 			}
52: 		case "O":
53: 			if isHighlight && blink {
54: 				sb.WriteString("\033[5m\033[32;1mO\033[0m")
55: 			} else {
56: 				sb.WriteString("\033[32;1mO\033[0m")
57: 			}
58: 		default:
59: 			sb.WriteString("\033[37m" + val + "\033[0m")
60: 		}
61: 
62: 		if i%3 != 2 {
63: 			sb.WriteString(" | ")
64: 		}
65: 	}
66: 
67: 	sb.WriteString("\n\n")
68: 	fmt.Print(sb.String())
69: }
70: 
71: func playerX(b [9]string) (int, [9]string) {
72: 	for {
73: 		fmt.Print("\033[31;1mPlayer X's turn. Enter a number (1-9): \033[0m")
74: 		reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)
75: 		input, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
76: 		input = strings.TrimSpace(input)
77: 
78: 		n, err := strconv.Atoi(input)
79: 		if err != nil || n < 1 || n > 9 {
80: 			fmt.Println("Invalid input. Enter a number between 1 and 9.")
81: 			continue
82: 		}
83: 
84: 		idx := n - 1
85: 		if b[idx] != "X" && b[idx] != "O" {
86: 			newBoard := b
87: 			newBoard[idx] = "X"
88: 			return idx, newBoard
89: 		}
90: 		fmt.Println("Space already taken. Choose another.")
91: 	}
92: }
93: 
94: func playerO(b [9]string) (int, [9]string) {
95: 	for {
96: 		fmt.Print("\033[32;1mPlayer O's turn. Enter a number (1-9): \033[0m")
97: 		reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)
98: 		input, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
99: 		input = strings.TrimSpace(input)
100: 
101: 		n, err := strconv.Atoi(input)
102: 		if err != nil || n < 1 || n > 9 {
103: 			fmt.Println("Invalid input. Enter a number between 1 and 9.")
104: 			continue
105: 		}
106: 
107: 		idx := n - 1
108: 		if b[idx] != "X" && b[idx] != "O" {
109: 			newBoard := b
110: 			newBoard[idx] = "O"
111: 			return idx, newBoard
112: 		}
113: 		fmt.Println("Space already taken. Choose another.")
114: 	}
115: }
116: 
117: func checkWinner(b [9]string) (int, []int) {
118: 	lines := [][3]int{
119: 		{0, 1, 2}, {3, 4, 5}, {6, 7, 8}, // rows
120: 		{0, 3, 6}, {1, 4, 7}, {2, 5, 8}, // cols
121: 		{0, 4, 8}, {2, 4, 6}, // diags
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	for _, line := range lines {
125: 		if b[line[0]] != "" && b[line[0]] == b[line[1]] && b[line[1]] == b[line[2]] {
126: 			player := 0
127: 			if b[line[0]] == "X" {
128: 				player = 1
129: 			} else {
130: 				player = 2
131: 			}
132: 			return player, []int{line[0], line[1], line[2]}
133: 		}
134: 	}
135: 	return 0, nil
136: }
137: 
138: func checkDraw(b [9]string) bool {
139: 	for _, v := range b {
140: 		if v == "X" || v == "O" {
141: 			continue
142: 		}
143: 		return false
144: 	}
145: 	return true
146: }
147: 
148: func playGame(sc *scores) {
149: 	var board [9]string
150: 	for i := 0; i < 9; i++ {
151: 		board[i] = strconv.Itoa(i + 1)
152: 	}
153: 
154: 	clearScreen()
155: 	renderBoard(board, *sc, nil, false)
156: 
157: 	for {
158: 		_, board = playerX(board)
159: 		clearScreen()
160: 		renderBoard(board, *sc, nil, false)
161: 
162: 		winner, winCells := checkWinner(board)
163: 		if winner == 1 {
164: 			sc.X++
165: 			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
166: 			clearScreen()
167: 			renderBoard(board, *sc, winCells, true)
168: 			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
169: 			fmt.Printf("\n\033[31;1mGame Over! X wins!\033[0m\n\n")
170: 			return
171: 		}
172: 
173: 		if checkDraw(board) {
174: 			sc.Draws++
175: 			clearScreen()
176: 			renderBoard(board, *sc, nil, false)
177: 			fmt.Printf("\n\033[37mGame Over! It's a draw!\033[0m\n\n")
178: 			return
179: 		}
180: 
181: 		_, board = playerO(board)
182: 		clearScreen()
183: 		renderBoard(board, *sc, nil, false)
184: 
185: 		winner, winCells = checkWinner(board)
186: 		if winner == 2 {
187: 			sc.O++
188: 			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
189: 			clearScreen()
190: 			renderBoard(board, *sc, winCells, true)
191: 			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
192: 			fmt.Printf("\n\033[32;1mGame Over! O wins!\033[0m\n\n")
193: 			return
194: 		}
195: 
196: 		if checkDraw(board) {
197: 			sc.Draws++
198: 			clearScreen()
199: 			renderBoard(board, *sc, nil, false)
200: 			fmt.Printf("\n\033[37mGame Over! It's a draw!\033[0m\n\n")
201: 			return
202: 		}
203: 	}
204: }
205: 
206: func main() {
207: 	var sc scores
208: 
209: 	for {
210: 		playGame(&sc)
211: 
212: 		fmt.Print("Play again? (y/n): ")
213: 		reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)
214: 		input, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
215: 		input = strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(input))
216: 
217: 		if input != "y" {
218: 			fmt.Println("Thanks for playing!")
219: 			break
220: 		}
221: 
222: 		clearScreen()
223: 	}
224: }
